name: default_of_credit_card_clients
citation: Yeh, I. C., & Lien, C. H. (2009). The comparisons of data mining techniques for the predictive accuracy of probability of default of credit card clients. Expert Systems with Applications, 36(2), 2473-2480.

columns_deliberately_excluded.reason: Fair-lending practice: never use protected/proxy-for-protected attributes as risk-model inputs. Not used at training time or inference time; demographic parity is instead monitored on model *outputs* (see portfolio.py fairness monitors).

license_note: Public UCI ML Repository research dataset, anonymized individual credit-card accounts (Taiwan, 2005). Used here as an honestly-labeled public proxy for a default outcome, not as real MSME/Indian data. No IDBI, bank, or third-party hackathon-team data of any kind is included.
why_this_dataset: No public dataset of real Indian MSME GST/UPI/EPFO alternate-data with an observed default outcome exists -- that gap is exactly what the IDBI sandbox (post-shortlisting) is for. This dataset is used only to validate a real outcome-label training pipeline via the 3-feature universal risk bridge documented in feature_bridge.py, and must be replaced by real IDBI sandbox repayment outcomes before any production use.
